heading: Definitions
label: 4507 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe Energy Conservation and Existing Buildings Act of 1976, referred to in par. (3)(A), probably means the Energy Conservation and Existing Buildings Act of 1976, which is title IV of , , . Part A of the Energy Conservation and Existing Buildings Act of 1976, is classified generally to part A (§ 6861 et seq.) of subchapter III of chapter 81 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. Parts D and G of title III of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act are classified generally to parts B (§ 6321 et seq.) and E (§ 6371 et seq.), respectively, of subchapter III of chapter 77 of Title 42. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Energy.

The term “subpart V regulations” means the provisions of Subpart V—Special Procedures for Distribution of Refunds (10 CFR 205.280–205.288) and any amendment made after , and all precedents and decisions under such regulations, but only to the extent that such provisions, precedents, decisions, and amendments are consistent with the provisions of this chapter.October 21, 1986

children.label: A
children.text: the program under part A of the Energy Conservation and Existing Buildings Act of 1976 ( and following);42 U.S.C. 6861

children.label: B
children.text: the programs under part D of title III of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act (relating to primary and supplemental State energy conservation programs; and following);42 U.S.C. 6321

children.label: C
children.text: the program under part G of title III of the Energy Policy and Conservation Act (relating to energy conservation for schools and hospitals; and following); and42 U.S.C. 6371

children.label: D
children.text: the program under the National Energy Extension Service Act ( and following).42 U.S.C. 7001

The term “person” includes refiners, retailers, resellers, farmer cooperatives, transportation entities, public and private utilities, school districts, Federal, State, and local governmental entities, farmers, and other individuals and their successors.
The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, the commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and any territory or possession of the United States.
